title: Golden-image test harness with pinned container
short_description: Build the end-to-end golden-image rig: pinned Docker container published to GHCR, baseline generate/compare/update commands, vendored anti-aliasing-aware YIQ tolerance comparison, addressable GoldenBaseline entities, and one trivial Skia-rendered fixture passing in CI.
long_description_md:
# Golden-image test harness with pinned container

## Why this task exists

This task delivers the renderer trust infrastructure required by **Phase 0: Foundations & determinism rig**. anima-ai's central promise is that a verified scene graph can be trusted without a person or model watching the resulting video. That promise begins below the scene-graph layer: every renderer primitive and style-pack element must be checked against a known-good raster result in a reproducible environment.

The founding research explicitly rejects relying on native developer-machine output for canonical baselines. Skia, bundled FreeType, system graphics libraries, and anti-aliasing behavior can shift between platforms or dependency versions even when nominal rendering inputs are identical. Canonical PNGs therefore come only from a pinned Linux container, while a small, vendored YIQ perceptual comparator handles harmless anti-aliasing variation during local comparison. This follows the product and architecture decision that output is bit-exact for a pinned platform and version, tolerance-equal across supported developer environments.

This task depends on `repo-scaffold-ci` because it consumes the package skeleton, Linux hash-locked dependencies, pytest configuration, and GitHub Actions foundation created there. It is intentionally completed before the Phase 1 renderer: the only rendering introduced here is a minimal direct-Skia fixture proving that image generation, baseline lookup, comparison, failure artifacts, container execution, and CI wiring all work end to end.

## What this task builds

### 1. Reproducible golden container

Add a committed Dockerfile and supporting configuration for a CPU-only Linux golden environment. The base image must be pinned by digest, the Python/native dependency set must come from the repository's hash-locked Linux requirements, and render-affecting ambient settings such as locale and timezone must be fixed. The image must contain the system libraries required by the pinned `skia-python` wheel but must not depend on host fonts, host graphics drivers, or runtime downloads.

A GitHub Actions workflow publishes immutable builds to GHCR using `GITHUB_TOKEN` with least-privilege package permissions. Published images receive an immutable source-revision tag, and the resolved digest is exposed by the workflow and smoke-pulled before the publish job succeeds. Golden CI and baseline metadata consume a full `tag@sha256:digest` reference, never a mutable tag such as `latest`. Container upgrades are explicit: rebuild, record the new digest, regenerate baselines, review the PNG changes, and commit the digest and baseline updates together.

### 2. Addressable GoldenBaseline entities

Implement a repository-internal typed `GoldenBaseline` model and deterministic registry/manifest. Each baseline has a stable, human-readable ID, fixture callable, canonicalized parameters, dimensions, committed PNG path, comparison tolerance, and the exact container image reference that generated it. Duplicate IDs, missing PNGs, orphan PNGs, invalid paths, non-canonical parameters, or metadata/image mismatches fail validation rather than being repaired or renamed implicitly.

The stable ID is the unit accepted by command filtering and reported in failures, so future primitives and style-pack assets remain individually selectable and diffable instead of becoming an anonymous directory of screenshots. Manifest serialization must be stable and sorted to produce useful source-control diffs.

### 3. Baseline workflow and vendored comparison

Provide runnable `generate`, `compare`, and `update` commands under a repository-owned Python module, with `--all` and repeatable `--id` selection:

- `generate` renders selected fixtures into a gitignored working directory without mutating committed baselines.
- `compare` checks generated images against the addressed committed baselines, exits nonzero on unacceptable differences, and writes deterministic actual/diff/report artifacts that identify the baseline ID, differing-pixel counts and ratios, threshold, dimensions, and artifact paths.
- `update` atomically promotes selected generated images and metadata into the committed baseline set. It must refuse to update canonical baselines unless it is running under the configured pinned golden image reference; local machines may generate and compare candidates but cannot silently bless them.

Vendor the comparison implementation in anima-ai rather than adding a thinly maintained image-snapshot wrapper. Normalize supported PNG inputs deterministically, reject dimension mismatches, calculate perceptual color distance in YIQ space, and detect/exclude anti-aliased edge pixels using neighboring-pixel brightness gradients. Unit fixtures must prove that identical images pass, allowable edge anti-aliasing variation passes, material interior/color changes fail, dimension mismatches fail, and a failing comparison emits an inspectable diff image. No VLM, network call, or nondeterministic image analysis is permitted.

### 4. End-to-end Skia proof in CI

Register one deliberately trivial, text-free Skia fixture—such as a fixed-size canvas containing a filled geometric shape and stroked path—with a committed baseline produced by the pinned container. Text is excluded because bundling and licensing launch-pack fonts is a separate Phase 0 task.

Add a golden-image GitHub Actions workflow that runs in the GHCR image pinned by digest, generates candidates, compares every registered baseline, and uploads actual images, diff PNGs, and the machine-readable comparison report when the job fails. The workflow must never update baselines automatically. A green run for the trivial fixture is the acceptance proof that the full path works: committed fixture → pinned Skia stack → generated PNG → addressable baseline → AA-aware comparison → CI result.

## Explicitly out of scope

- The Phase 1 scene graph, geometry resolver, animation system, production renderer, frame cache, or video encoding pipeline.
- Seeded procedural wobble and its RNG service, which are separate Phase 0/Phase 3 work.
- Launch-pack font selection, bundling, license vetting, text shaping fixtures, or font baselines.
- Golden coverage for real primitives, maps, characters, textures, and style packs; those later phases must register their coverage through this harness.
- Cross-platform bit-for-bit equality. Developer machines use the documented tolerance comparator; canonical baseline generation remains pinned to the GHCR Linux image.

## Acceptance shape

Done when the immutable container has been published and smoke-pulled from GHCR, its digest is pinned in both CI and baseline metadata, the registry and three commands are tested, the vendored YIQ/anti-aliasing comparator passes its positive and negative cases, and the trivial direct-Skia fixture generates and compares successfully in the pinned-container CI job. Intentionally changing the fixture must make CI fail with useful actual/diff/report artifacts, while baseline replacement remains an explicit pinned-container-only operation.

deliverables.description: Digest-pinned CPU-only Linux golden Dockerfile, built from a digest-pinned base and the hash-locked Linux dependency set, containing the system libraries the pinned skia-python wheel needs with no host font/driver/runtime-download dependence. Verified with a local docker build and a smoke run of the resulting image, since the project has no git repo or GitHub remote yet and GHCR publish cannot be exercised locally.

verification.command: docker build -t anima-golden:local -f docker/golden.Dockerfile . && docker run --rm anima-golden:local python -c "import skia; print('skia import OK in golden container')"

deliverables.description: Least-privilege GitHub Actions workflow file (.github/workflows/golden-container.yml) that publishes immutable source-revision-tagged builds of the golden Dockerfile to GHCR using GITHUB_TOKEN, reports the resolved digest, and smoke-pulls that digest before succeeding; actually publishing to GHCR is an external, irreversible action under Arnav's GitHub account and must be flagged to him first per COFOUNDER_PERSONA.md.

verification.steps: Flag to Arnav first per COFOUNDER_PERSONA.md before publishing any image to GHCR or granting GITHUB_TOKEN package-write permissions. Confirm .github/workflows/golden-container.yml exists, targets least-privilege permissions (packages: write only), tags images with the immutable source revision (not `latest`), and prints the resolved digest. Once approved and the repo/remote exist (see repo-scaffold-ci's flagged repo-creation deliverable), let the workflow run on push, then confirm in the GitHub UI or via `gh run view` that the publish job succeeded, the digest was smoke-pulled, and record the resulting `tag@sha256:digest` reference for use in CI and baseline metadata.

deliverables.description: Vendored deterministic PNG comparator implementing YIQ perceptual distance and neighboring-pixel anti-aliasing detection, with configurable thresholds, strict dimension checking, stable metrics, and diff-image generation; tests cover identical images, tolerated AA-edge variation, material pixel changes, and size mismatches.

Typed, manifest-backed GoldenBaseline registry with stable IDs, canonical parameters, PNG paths, tolerances, and pinned container references, together with tested generate/compare/update commands supporting --all and --id selection; update is atomic and refuses to bless baselines outside the configured pinned container.

One committed, text-free direct-Skia fixture registered as a GoldenBaseline, with its committed baseline PNG checked in. Verified two ways since there is no GitHub remote to run a real workflow against yet: (1) locally, inside the golden Docker image, generate the fixture's candidate into the gitignored working directory and then run compare-only against the already-committed baseline PNG (never regenerating the committed baseline itself); (2) the .github/workflows/goldens.yml file content is asserted to run generate-then-compare in the pinned-digest GHCR image, upload actual/diff/report artifacts on failure, and never update baselines.

verification.command: docker build -t anima-golden:local -f docker/golden.Dockerfile . && docker run --rm -v "$(pwd)/tests/golden/baselines:/workspace/tests/golden/baselines:ro" anima-golden:local sh -c "python -m anima.testing.goldens generate --id trivial-shape-fixture && python -m anima.testing.goldens compare --id trivial-shape-fixture" && python -c "import yaml; d = yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/goldens.yml')); assert d, 'workflow YAML did not parse'" && grep -q "generate" .github/workflows/goldens.yml && grep -q "compare" .github/workflows/goldens.yml && ! grep -q "testing.goldens update" .github/workflows/goldens.yml && grep -q "upload-artifact" .github/workflows/goldens.yml && grep -q "sha256:" .github/workflows/goldens.yml
